# LinkedIn Draft - 2026-06-17

Anthropic's Claude models were taken offline due to conflict with the US government over the Fable 5 jailbreak and export control classification.

Went through 19 links today. Here's what made the brief:

1. Anthropic's Claude models went offline due to US government policy clash over Fable jailbreak
   Direct impact on Claude API availability — affects any builder running Claude-dependent automation or agents.

2. Vercel Functions can now run up to 30 minutes
   Changes the calculus for long-running tasks on Vercel — previously you'd need a queue/worker setup to stay under the old limit.

3. datasette-agent 0.3a0: execute_write_sql with user approval
   Human approval pattern for agent DB writes is directly applicable to MCP server design for agentic workflows.

4. A backdoor in a LinkedIn job offer
   Real documented attack vector targeting developers via LinkedIn — high risk for solo devs who review code for freelance or contract work.

5. Cloudflare CAPTCHA triggered by URLs containing ampersands
   Practical gotcha that would be hard to debug without knowing the cause — saves hours of debugging time.

Full brief with all 19 researched links → https://thenightlylibrarian.com/briefs/2026-06-17
